basics.name: Aditya Sripada
basics.label: Staff Robotics Engineer & Robotics Systems Architect
basics.summary: I make robots work — not just in the lab, but at scale, in production, every day. Staff Robotics Engineer at Nimble.ai, focused on autonomous mobile manipulation for warehouse operations. MS in Robotics from Carnegie Mellon University's Robotics Institute.

work.name: Nimble.ai
work.position: Staff Robotics Engineer & Robotics Systems Architect
highlights: Enabling autonomous mobile manipulator fleets that move, carry, pick and pack millions of SKUs across warehouses.

work.name: Nimble.ai
work.summary: Tech Lead on Mobile Manipulation Robotics Team
highlights: Spearheaded development of high-impact control features, doubling throughput in Q4 2023, which proved crucial for commercial success and fundraising.
Diagnosed and resolved critical production issues involving mobile robot dynamics slip and sensing noise across heterogeneous robot fleets.
Designed simulation and data analytics tools to benchmark and improve locomotion reliability and multi-sensor integration.
Collaborated with hardware, software, and production teams to deliver robust system improvements under high operational load.

work.name: Nimble.ai
highlights: First robotics engineer on the flagship robotic system, responsible for item picking and autonomous warehouse navigation.

work.name: Carnegie Mellon University Robotics Institute
highlights: Worked on optimal control of highly non-linear, dynamic, and underactuated legged systems, including the ATRIAS biped.
Focused on exploiting natural dynamics of legged systems to achieve dynamic maneuvers with minimal energy expenditure.

work.name: Carnegie Mellon University
work.position: State Estimation & Localization, CMU Moon Rover
highlights: Developed robust state estimator for localization of the CMU Moonranger autonomous space rover, targeting the Lunar South Pole.
Developed vehicle kinematics models and probabilistic filters for localization.
Designed power switching circuitry to mitigate single event upsets caused by ionizing radiation in space.

work.name: Indian Institute of Technology, Hyderabad
work.position: Research Engineer, Robotics and Intelligent Systems Laboratory
highlights: Engineered a full-sized humanoid robot (1.72m) from scratch with a budget of INR 7M (USD 100K).
Developed controllers for walking, active balance, push recovery, and vertical jumping.
Built a flying biped combining terrain capabilities of a biped with aerial capabilities of a quadrotor (presented at IROS 2018).
Designed novel footplates for prostheses and orthoses enhancing lateral stability (patented).
Implemented micron-level precision control on a five-bar parallel manipulator.

work.name: LVPEI Center for Innovation
work.summary: Research collaboration between LVPEI Center for Innovation and MIT Media Lab
highlights: Gaze tracking of infants to quantify response to changing light stimuli without subjectivity.
Developed hardware system for Pupil+, a handheld device to quantify pupillary and neurological responses to light stimuli.
Developed an inexpensive, multifunctional device to diagnose Dry Eye Syndrome.
Technical mentor at the 5th and 6th editions of 'Engineering the Eye' Hackathon; mentored teams placed 1st runner-up both years.

work.name: SRM Team Humanoid, SRM University
highlights: Led design, fabrication, and control of humanoid robots for international competitions.
Won India's first Gold Medal at Robogames 2017 in the Autonomous Humanoid Penalty Kick category.

courses: CMU 16-868: Biomechanics and Motor Control of Legged Locomotion
highlights: Thesis: Turning Behavior of Running Systems induced by Leg Placement
Research with Prof. Hartmut Geyer on optimal control of dynamic legged systems, including the ATRIAS biped
Partially supported by NSF Grant 1563807: Collaborative Research on Self-stabilizing Running Robots
Best Graduation Thesis, EEE Department (among 700+ students)
University Merit Scholar, all 4 years

awards.title: Mike Stilman Award for Outstanding Research Paper, Finalist
awards.awarder: IEEE RAS International Conference on Humanoids 2025, Seoul, South Korea
awards.summary: Competing against 80+ institutions from 20+ countries for the TARS3D project.

awards.title: Best Safety Features Award
awards.summary: Won among 400 contestants as the only one-person team. Designed the robot in under 3 days.

awards.title: Jury Member, Drones and Robotics Track
awards.awarder: Smart India Hackathon Hardware Edition, Ministry of Human Resource Development, India

awards.title: Gold Medal – Autonomous Humanoid Penalty Kick
awards.summary: India's first Robogames gold medal. Also won Silver (Humanoid Freestyle), Silver (Humanoid Biped Race), Bronze (RC Humanoid Sumo).

awards.title: Third Prize, Humanoid Application Challenge: Humanoid Magic
awards.awarder: IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems 2017

publications.name: Walking, Rolling, and Beyond: First-Principles and RL Locomotion on a TARS-Inspired Robot
publications.publisher: IEEE-RAS International Conference on Humanoid Robots 2025 (Humanoids 2025)

publications.name: Building Baymax: A First Step Toward Soft-Link Humanoids
Bridging Fiction and Reality: Evaluating the Feasibility of Adaptive Gaits Inspired by TARS from Interstellar
Adaptive Gaits of TARS: A Sci-Fi Inspired Multimodal Robot
Turning Behavior of Running Systems induced by Leg Placement
publications.publisher: Robotics Institute, Carnegie Mellon University, Technical Report
publications.summary: MS Thesis work with Prof. Hartmut Geyer.

publications.name: Biped Robot Vertical Jumping with Control Constraints
Real-Time Teleoperation of a Humanoid Robot with Motion Imitation and Legged Locomotion
publications.publisher: International Conference on Advanced Robotics and Mechatronics 2018 (ICARM 2018)

publications.name: Dynamic Lateral Balance of Humanoid Robots on Unstable Surfaces

references.name: Jasmeet Singh
references.reference: One of the hardest working people I know. His enthusiasm for robotics and humanoids is unparalleled by anyone else I've come across. This makes his passion shine through in his work and drives him to constantly learn and keep on improving his skills which is something I really admire about him.

references.name: Raghu Gullapalli
references.reference: Aditya was an amazing LVP-Mitra Fellow at the LVPEI Center for Innovation. His work ethic, grit and intellectual curiosity set a high benchmark for future cohorts of Fellows. He has been an invaluable alumni contributor and mentor. I am expecting great things from him, as he pursues his dreams in Humanoid Robotics development.

projects.name: TARS3D
projects.summary: A fully 3D-printed research robot inspired by TARS from the film Interstellar. 0.25m tall, 0.99kg. Walks at 0.10 m/s and rolls at 0.51 m/s using rimless-wheel dynamics — the first TARS replica that can both walk and roll. Mike Stilman Award finalist at Humanoids 2025.
highlights: 7 iterative prototypes built independently without funding or formal affiliation
Combines passive dynamic walking with RL-based locomotion
Presented at Humanoids 2025 and ICRA 2025

projects.name: Deadbeat Control for Terrain Blind Bipedal Running
projects.summary: Research on control policies for dynamic bipedal running without terrain sensing, exploiting natural system dynamics.

projects.name: IITH Humanoid (Charlie)
projects.summary: Full-sized humanoid robot (1.72m) built from scratch at IIT Hyderabad with a budget of INR 7M. Developed controllers for walking, balance, push recovery, and jumping.
